mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly more negative than baseline during rate-rising periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0% for two consecutive months, labor market tightness raises wage inflation risk, prompting expectations of further monetary tightening and depressing equity returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following sub-4% unemployment readings are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration of CPI inflation above 3% year-over-year alongside a rising 3-month federal funds rate trend signals an intensifying tightening cycle, causing equity markets to price in higher discount rates and lower earnings multiples over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month trend positive
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions are jointly satisfied, forward 20d SPX returns are not significantly negative relative to unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across two independent tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A greater than 5% three-month decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand, and this effect on forward equity returns is materially stronger and more negative when it occurs during a monetary tightening cycle than in other regimes.
failure_condition: The negative return effect of HOUST declines is not measurably stronger during tightening regimes than during easing or flat-rate regimes (difference in mean forward returns not significant at p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month reading in industrial production reflects improving real economic momentum that is incorporated into equity prices with a short lag, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no persistent short-horizon predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A retail sales month-over-month growth reading above 0.5% signals robust consumer spending momentum that supports corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following RSAFS growth above threshold are not significantly higher than the unconditional average (p > 0.05) across at least two distinct expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-like joint signal, where the unemployment rate rises 0.3 percentage points above its trailing 12-month low while nonfarm payroll growth simultaneously decelerates, indicates an emerging labor market downturn that precedes broader equity market weakness over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month low AND PAYEMS 3-month average growth decelerates
failure_condition: In historical instances where both conditions co-occur, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the joint signal fails to outperform either single-series signal in rank IC terms.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production year-over-year growth decelerates below 2% during a late-cycle slowdown regime, it signals fading manufacturing momentum consistent with an approaching growth peak, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns following sub-2% INDPRO YoY growth are not significantly negative relative to baseline during late-cycle periods (p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign outside recessionary transitions.
